normalized_definitions.experimental_test_summary: A high-level summary of the overall experimental procedure, including test type, solution chemistry, operating parameters, and environmental conditions.
normalized_definitions.pitting_potential: The specific extracted critical potential value, including units of measure, at which stable localized pitting initiation occurs on the material surface.
normalized_definitions.electrolyte_temperature: The explicitly controlled temperature of the test solution or electrolyte during the measurement, representing a critical thermodynamic variable that influences pitting susceptibility.
normalized_definitions.polarization_scan_rate: The continuous potential sweep rate or the discrete step-size and holding duration applied to change the electrical potential during potentiodynamic polarization testing.
normalized_definitions.reference_electrode: The standard reference electrode (e.g., SCE, Ag/AgCl) used during electrochemical testing to measure and standardize electrical potentials.
normalized_definitions.electrolyte_composition: The precise chemical formulation of the testing solution, including the specific solvent, molar concentrations of primary salts, and the specific chemical identity and exact concentration of any purposely added soluble inhibitors designed to interact with the material and suppress pitting.
normalized_definitions.aeration_status: The specific condition of dissolved reactive gases in the electrolyte, including whether the solution was aerated, naturally exposed to air, or deaerated/purged with an inert gas like nitrogen or argon.
normalized_definitions.electrolyte_pH: The quantitatively measured or explicitly controlled initial and/or final hydrogen ion concentration (pH) of the test solution, which dictates the thermodynamic stability of passive oxide films, dissolved species, active dissolution, and localized repassivation kinetics.
normalized_definitions.critical_pitting_temperature: The specifically extracted threshold temperature (T_pit) at which stable pitting corrosion initiates, serving as a primary and distinct measure of localized corrosion resistance alongside the pitting potential.
normalized_definitions.corrosion_potential: The measured open-circuit, free, or zero-current resting potential (Ecorr) established by the working electrode in the test solution at equilibrium, serving as the fundamental thermodynamic baseline prior to the application of an external anodic polarization sweep.
normalized_definitions.corrosion_current_density: The baseline uniform corrosion rate or active dissolution current density (icorr) measured in current per unit area, typically extracted from the Tafel region of the polarization curve.
normalized_definitions.polarization_potential_range: The specific starting (initial) and ending (final) electrical potentials applied during the potentiodynamic scan, establishing the boundary conditions of the test.
normalized_definitions.test_standard_protocol: The official standardized methodology or recognized industry standard (e.g., ASTM G59) adhered to for equipment calibration and execution of the electrochemical corrosion experiments.
normalized_definitions.ocp_stabilization_protocol: The explicit time duration, rest period, or stability criteria (e.g., potential fluctuation thresholds) applied prior to testing to ensure the specimen freely corrodes and reaches a steady-state open-circuit potential (OCP) electrochemical equilibrium with the environment before the polarization scan begins.
normalized_definitions.cathodic_preconditioning: The specific in situ electrochemical parameters, including the applied cathodic potential and its duration, used immediately prior to the polarization test to electrochemically reduce native air-formed surface oxides and ensure a highly standardized, reproducible starting surface state.
normalized_definitions.counter_electrode_details: The material composition, structural form, physical geometry, exposed area, and precise configuration (including isolation methods to prevent contamination) of the auxiliary/counter electrode used to complete the electrical circuit in the multi-electrode electrochemical cell.
normalized_definitions.repassivation_potential: The protection or repassivation potential (Erep/Eprot) identified during the reverse scan of a cyclic polarization test at the intersection of the forward and reversed branches, representing the critical thermodynamic threshold below which actively propagating localized pits arrest and current density returns to passive levels.
normalized_definitions.passive_current_density: The stable, steady-state anodic current density (ipass) measured within the passive potential range prior to localized pitting breakdown or transpassive dissolution, indicating the protective quality and dissolution rate of the passive film.
normalized_definitions.cell_geometry_and_electrode_spacing: The precisely measured physical distances and spatial configuration between the working, counter, and reference electrodes in the electrochemical cell, which dictate the uncompensated solution resistance (IR drop) that can skew true potential measurements.
normalized_definitions.pitting_current_density_threshold: The explicit current density criterion or threshold (e.g., 100 µA/cm²) utilized by researchers to quantitatively define and extract the critical pitting potential from the polarization curve.
normalized_definitions.exposed_working_electrode_area: The specific geometric surface area of the test specimen exposed to the electrolyte, which scales the measured current to current density and affects the statistical probability of capturing pitting defects.
normalized_definitions.critical_current_density: The maximum active dissolution current density (icrit) observed prior to the active-to-passive transition during the potentiodynamic sweep.
